summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a zoning workshop focused on potential text and map amendments to the zoning code, particularly in the Northeast and Southeast quadrants. Key discussions centered on separating Agricultural (AG) and R1 zoning districts, establishing a two-acre minimum lot size for R1 in the Northeast quadrant, addressing solar farm regulations in coordination with Clermont County Commissioners, and increasing minimum lot sizes and setback requirements in Planned Unit Developments (PUDs).
decisions: Motion to adopt the agenda passed
Direction given to pursue two-acre minimum lot size for R1 zoning in the Northeast quadrant
Direction given to separate AG and R1 zoning districts
Direction to add solar farm discussion to the next Zoning Commission meeting on February 7th
action_items: Director Holbert and staff to determine the best zoning label/approach for two-acre lots in the Northeast quadrant
Harry (Zoning) to add solar farm issue to the February 7th Zoning Commission meeting agenda
Fiscal Officer/staff to research legal authority on minimum lot sizes and AG zoning requirements under ORC
Staff to get direction from prosecutor's office on legality of five-acre and larger lot requirements and AG/R1 separation
Staff to examine PUD minimum lot size standards and side yard setback requirements for potential increases
key_amounts: 290 lots of 5 acres or more in the Northeast quadrant
14 lots in R3 zone district ranging from 1.8 to 25 acres
Current PUD minimum lot size is 5,000 square feet
R3 lots are approximately 12,800 square feet
Current side yard setback is 10 feet
Current front setback is 50 feet with discussion of increasing to 60 feet
Goshen Township AG standard is 5 acres; ORC AG standard is 10 acres
Proposed R1 minimum lot size of 2 acres in Northeast quadrant
notable_topics: Separation of Agricultural (AG) and R1 zoning districts
Two-acre minimum lot size for R1 in the Northeast quadrant
Solar farm regulation and Senate Bill 52 county authority
PUD lot size minimums and setback requirements
Northeast and Southeast quadrant zoning focus areas
Affordable housing concerns vs. overcrowding prevention
Agricultural preservation district and overlay district history
notes: Transcript contains significant amounts of repeated filler text ('Board of Trustees Meeting. Clermont County, Ohio 45122. Roads.') and garbled/corrupted text sections, making portions difficult to parse. The video title says 2022 but the transcript states January 31st 2023 at 6 PM. The meeting is the second part of a two-part recording. Discussion referenced Talbert Farm subdivision as an example of successful two-acre development, and Angelwood subdivision as an example of R3 development. The Zoning Commission's next meeting was noted as February 7th.
title: BOT 1.31.22 2 of 2.mp4
